description: PATCH-style JSON endpoints (PUT /api/numbers/:id, PUT /api/settings) could never clear optional fields like agentId: zod z.string().optional() rejects null, JSON.stringify drops undefined client-side, and db Object.assign leaves absent keys intact — so a number-to-agent mapping was permanent
resolution: Accept z.string().nullable().optional(); when the key is present, set patch.field = value || undefined explicitly (key present with undefined) so Object.assign overwrites and JSON persistence drops it; frontend sends null (not undefined) to clear. Also validate non-empty ids against the store (400 on unknown). Fixed in a821962 with tests in tests/store-api.test.ts

name: dual-runner API tests
description: API tests must pass under both 'tsx --test' (npm test) and 'bun test': bun's node:test shim throws NotImplementedError on nested t.test() subtests, so write flat top-level test() calls with a memoized async init() for shared setup; point DATA_DIR at a mkdtempSync dir BEFORE dynamic-importing src/store/db (config reads env at import), and server.unref() the express listener so the process can exit

bun test runs ALL test files in one process, so src/config (parsed from process.env at first import) is cached with whichever test file's env won the import race; a later file's process.env.SARVAM_BASE_URL assignment is silently ignored and its mock server never gets hit (5s handshake timeouts). The whole-suite bun run was already red before this session (engine/server/webhooks cross-talk); npm test (tsx, process-per-file) is the green canonical runner.
resolution: In the test file's before(), after the dynamic import, also import the cached config object and patch the fields the module reads live (e.g. config.sarvam.baseUrl/apiKey) — order-immune under both runners. See tests/stt-stream.test.ts.
